messages.content: Hey guys! Random thought—what were your favorite games to play as a kid? I was feeling nostalgic today 😄
Ooo love this question! I used to play hide and seek all the time, especially at family get-togethers. We'd get super competitive 😂 Also, anyone else remember those epic rounds of tag at recess?

Haha yes! Hide and seek was a classic. I also loved playing kickball in the neighborhood—felt like half the block would join in. Oh, and four square at school! Did anyone else have that one kid who took it WAY too seriously? 😂

Haha absolutely, there was always that one four square champ who made up crazy rules just to win 😂 I totally forgot about kickball! We used to play dodgeball in gym class too, and it got intense. Anyone else ever get hit right in the face and pretend it didn’t hurt?

Oh man, dodgeball was brutal! I definitely took a few to the face and tried to play it cool 😂 Anyone else ever play Red Rover? That one always ended with someone getting clotheslined or just giving up and laughing on the ground. And I was obsessed with marbles for a while too, but I could never win any of the cool ones!

Red Rover! I haven’t thought about that in forever 😂 I swear someone always went flying every round. And marbles—I had a tiny bag of them but never really figured out the strategy, just liked how shiny they were. Did you guys ever play hopscotch or jacks? Those were my go-tos when I wanted a break from the chaos!

Oh yeah, hopscotch! That was a go-to during recess when I didn’t feel like running around. Never really mastered jacks though—my coordination was seriously lacking 😂 Did you all ever play “Mother May I?” or “Simon Says”? I always tried to come up with the trickiest instructions when it was my turn to be in charge.

Oh man, “Mother May I?” brings back memories! I’d always mess up and forget to say “Mother may I?” and get sent back to the start 😅 Simon Says was fun too—I used to get so competitive and try to catch my friends slipping up. Did you guys ever play “Capture the Flag” at summer camp or in the park? That was my absolute favorite, especially at dusk when it felt all sneaky and intense!

Capture the Flag was the BEST! I loved the chaos when everyone would just start sprinting in different directions. We used to play at dusk too and it felt so epic, like we were on some secret mission 😂 Did anyone else ever play flashlight tag at night? That game made me feel like a ninja even if I was just hiding behind a bush!

Flashlight tag was SO much fun! I remember getting way too into it and trying to blend into the shadows like I was in some spy movie 😂 The adrenaline rush when the flashlight beam got close was real. Did anyone ever play “Ghost in the Graveyard”? We’d always end up scaring ourselves way more than necessary and then no one wanted to walk home alone after haha.

Oh my gosh, Ghost in the Graveyard used to freak me out so much but I loved it! There was always that one kid who’d hide in the creepiest spot and jump out screaming just to get everyone running. 😅 Anyone remember Sardines? It was like reverse hide and seek—once you found the person hiding, you’d squish in with them until everyone was packed in together. So silly but so much fun!

Sardines was hilarious, especially when you ended up stuck in the weirdest places with everyone trying not to laugh! Honestly though, my favorite was always jump rope—either solo or double dutch if we could convince enough people to help swing. I could spend ages trying to nail all the different rhymes and tricks. Did you guys have any favorite jump rope chants?

Oh man, jump rope rhymes were the best! I mostly remember the “Cinderella, dressed in yella…” one, and we’d mess it up on purpose just to start over and laugh. Double dutch always looked so cool but I swear I had two left feet—never quite got the timing down 😂 Did you ever play Chinese jump rope too? We’d use those stretchy loops and try to get the pattern just right. So much coordination for little kid games!

Yes! Chinese jump rope was so much fun—though I’d always forget the patterns halfway through and just make up some random moves 😂 It’s wild how creative we got with just a piece of string or a couple of ropes. Did you guys ever get into hand-clapping games? Like “Miss Mary Mack” or “Down Down Baby”? We would do those for ages on the playground whenever we needed a break from running around.

Yes! “Miss Mary Mack” and “Down Down Baby” were playground staples. I feel like we had a new hand-clapping game every week—someone would always come back from visiting cousins or a different school and teach us a new one. My brain still remembers all the patterns somehow 😂 Did you ever get sucked into those endless rounds of “Pat-a-cake” with younger cousins or siblings? My hands would always end up red from going too fast!

Oh totally! My little cousins would get obsessed with Pat-a-cake and make me play like a million times in a row. I’d try to speed it up just to see if they could keep up, and then someone would end up giggling too hard to keep going. 😂 Did you guys ever play “Heads Up, Seven Up” during indoor recess? That game always felt so mysterious—I’d peek at people’s shoes and still somehow always guess wrong!

Heads Up, Seven Up was the best rainy day game! I was always convinced I had a foolproof shoe-spotting strategy, but nope, never worked out for me either 😂 Indoor recess always brought out the classics—did you ever play Four Corners? It got so intense, especially when everyone tried to fake each other out at the last second. Honestly, it made being stuck inside way more fun than I expected!

Four Corners was such a classic! I loved trying to quietly sneak to another corner at the last second and hope nobody noticed. 😂 Indoor recess was where all the sneaky strategizing happened! Did anyone ever play “Silent Ball”? The teacher would give us a soft ball and we’d have to toss it around without making a sound—if you talked or dropped it, you were out. It was surprisingly hard, especially for a group of kids who couldn’t stop giggling!

Oh yeah, Silent Ball! That was the ultimate test of not cracking up—I’d always get the giggles and be out in the first round 😂 I remember sometimes we’d end up with a paper ball if nothing else was around. Did anyone else play “Seven Up” with cards? I think we made up half the rules as we went, but it was a go-to for killing time during rainy days. It’s wild how creative we got just to keep ourselves entertained!

Haha yes! We totally made up card games too—sometimes I think the rules changed every time we played, depending on who was winning 😅 And if all else failed, we’d just play endless rounds of Uno or Crazy Eights. I swear, some people have a hidd
